items.author: ProductMindset
items.text: Transformed how our team runs ideation sessions. The structured prompting chains surface angles we'd never think of on our own.

items.author: startup_cto_diary
items.text: Solid for early-stage feature scoping. Sometimes the suggestions are a bit generic but prompting it with constraints sharpens the output.

items.author: UXResearcher_Kai
items.text: Used this to prepare stakeholder workshops — the divergent-thinking mode is genuinely impressive and saves hours of prep.

items.author: fullstack_felix
items.text: Probably the best coding assistant I've used. It catches subtle bugs in TypeScript generics that other tools miss entirely.

items.author: backend_beatriz
items.text: The refactoring suggestions respect the existing architecture rather than rewriting everything from scratch — huge win on legacy codebases.

items.author: weekend_hacker_99
items.text: Does exactly what it says. Occasionally verbose in explanations when you just want the code, but overall excellent quality.

items.author: SeniorDevSam
items.text: Pair programming at 3am when my team is asleep. Understands context across files better than anything else I've tested.

items.author: LegalEagle_Marco
items.text: Extracts clause-level detail from contracts that would take a paralegal hours to manually review. Game-changer for due diligence work.

items.author: research_librarian
items.text: Good at summarizing dense academic papers. Struggles with multi-column layouts and footnotes in older scanned PDFs.

items.author: FinanceAnalystPro
items.text: Works well for financial reports. I'd love better table extraction, but the narrative summarization is excellent.

items.author: SREoncall
items.text: This is the plugin that makes complex incident postmortems tractable. It forces a step-by-step causal chain that I now include in every RCA.

items.author: architekt_anna
items.text: Invaluable for system design sessions. Forces you to think through dependencies before committing to an approach.

items.author: pmthoughts
items.text: It's useful but can feel slow for simpler problems. Best reserved for genuinely complex multi-step reasoning tasks.

items.author: researchbot_ryan
items.text: Solid web search integration. The source attribution makes it usable for anything where you need to cite your findings.

items.author: competitive_intel_ops
items.text: Combined with brainstorming plugin it gives you a research-to-ideation pipeline that's hard to beat for market analysis.

items.author: techwriter_tomas
items.text: Results are relevant and the summaries are concise. Occasionally surfaces outdated pages but generally reliable for current topics.

items.author: PythonEngineer_Dev
items.text: Strong for Python and JavaScript. Autocomplete accuracy in complex async patterns is noticeably better than older models.

items.author: GoDevGunnar
items.text: Works well for most languages. Go support has improved considerably — it now produces idiomatic error handling most of the time.

items.author: mobiledev_mia
items.text: Very capable. Pair it with GitHub plugin for review automation and the value compound quickly.

items.author: DevOpsDaily
items.text: PR review workflow is seamlessly integrated. The AI diff summaries alone save 30 minutes per day across our team.

items.author: open_source_otto
items.text: Issue triage and labeling automation is excellent. Finally cleared a 6-month-old backlog of unlabeled issues in one afternoon.

items.author: release_engineer_ren
items.text: Changelog generation from commit history is very good. Occasionally over-includes minor dependency bumps but easily filtered.

items.author: CodingCatalina
items.text: The code review suggestions are context-aware and catch things static analysis misses. Worth it for larger teams alone.

items.author: MLEngineer_Priya
items.text: Model search and download right from the CLI without leaving my dev environment. The dataset preview feature is underrated.

items.author: data_scientist_dom
items.text: Excellent for rapid model prototyping. Inference API integration is smooth though rate limits bite on heavy experimentation days.

items.author: ResearchEngineer_Rin
items.text: Combined with the Anthropic coding plugin for eval scripting this becomes a genuinely powerful ML research assistant.

items.author: backend_beatriz
items.text: Solid boilerplate generation for REST APIs. The OpenAPI spec output saves hours of documentation work on each new service.

items.author: microservices_mike
items.text: Good understanding of service-to-service auth patterns. Correctly suggested JWT rotation strategy for our inter-service calls.

items.author: api_architect_al
items.text: Useful, though database schema design suggestions could be more opinionated about normalization trade-offs.

items.author: ReactDevRuby
items.text: Component generation is fast and the output follows modern React patterns. Custom hook suggestions in particular are excellent.

items.author: css_wizard_wei
items.text: Good at Tailwind and CSS Modules. Sometimes suggests deprecated patterns for older frameworks but current tooling support is great.

items.author: a11y_advocate_ama
items.text: Accessibility considerations are better than most tools — it proactively adds ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation in generated components.

items.author: indie_hacker_ingrid
items.text: This cuts MVP time in half. The full-stack scaffolding with auth, database, and API wired together out of the box is incredibly practical.

items.author: solofounder_sol
items.text: Next.js + Prisma stack setup is really clean. Deployment config for Vercel works first-try which is more than I can say for most tools.

items.author: startup_cto_diary
items.text: Saved us from setting up boilerplate for the third time. Opinionated in a good way.

items.author: DevOpsDaily
items.text: Terraform and Helm generation are both solid. The multi-environment promotion workflow it scaffolded matches our internal best practices almost exactly.

items.author: k8s_whisperer_ko
items.text: Kubernetes manifest generation with proper resource limits and health checks — no more copy-pasting from previous projects.

items.author: sre_siddarth
items.text: Good for CI/CD pipeline generation. GitHub Actions support is excellent; Bitbucket support feels like an afterthought.

items.author: aiProductBuilder_ab
items.text: The RAG pipeline scaffolding is excellent — chunking strategies, embedding model selection, retrieval scoring all covered with sensible defaults.

items.author: MLEngineer_Priya
items.text: Best plugin I've found for building LLM-powered tools. Evaluation harness generation alone is worth the install.

items.author: promptEngineer_pete
items.text: Prompt engineering patterns are well-chosen. Would love native LangGraph support but the general agent loop scaffolding works.

items.author: ResearchEngineer_Rin
items.text: Saved our team from reinventing the observability wheel for LLM apps. Tracing integration suggestions were spot-on.

items.author: mobiledev_mia
items.text: React Native generation is good. Expo workflow is well-understood and the navigation scaffold follows Expo Router conventions correctly.

items.author: ios_eng_ivan
items.text: Reasonable for cross-platform work. Native Swift/Kotlin support is thin — stick to it for React Native projects.

items.author: android_dev_ariel
items.text: Handles push notification setup better than any tool I've used before. Saves the painful back-and-forth with docs.

items.author: data_engineer_dani
items.text: dbt model generation is exceptional. The lineage-aware refactoring suggestions alone justify the install for any warehouse project.

items.author: data_scientist_dom
items.text: Good Spark job scaffolding. I especially like that it reasons about partition strategies and not just syntax.

items.author: analytics_eng_alex
items.text: Solid overall. Airflow DAG generation could handle more complex dependency graphs but simpler pipelines come out clean.

items.author: appsec_engineer_ash
items.text: The OWASP Top 10 scan integration is thorough and the remediation suggestions include code examples — not just descriptions of the problem.

items.author: pentest_practitioner
items.text: Found two SQL injection vectors in a codebase that had passed our standard linting rules. Very impressed with the depth of analysis.

items.author: cloud_sec_cora
items.text: Good for IaC security review. Catches overly permissive IAM policies reliably. False positive rate is low enough for daily use.

items.author: pmthoughts
items.text: PRD drafting support is genuinely useful. It asks the right clarifying questions before writing rather than hallucinating requirements.

items.author: ProductMindset
items.text: User story generation with acceptance criteria saves at least two hours of sprint planning prep per week for our team.

items.author: growth_pm_grace
items.text: Competitive analysis framework support is solid. Could use deeper integration with metrics and OKR tooling.

items.author: creative_director_cleo
items.text: Different flavor than the Anthropic brainstorming plugin — leans more creative and lateral. Good for concept exploration in design sprints.

items.author: UXResearcher_Kai
items.text: The "superpowers" framing encourages more ambitious thinking. I use it alongside the Anthropic version for a wider range of ideas.

items.author: indie_hacker_ingrid
items.text: Fun and surprising output. Best for early exploration when you want to break out of obvious solutions.

items.author: fullstack_felix
items.text: Interesting alternative perspective on code problems. Sometimes suggests unconventional approaches that turn out to be elegant solutions.

items.author: SeniorDevSam
items.text: When I'm stuck on an algorithmic problem this is my second opinion. Found a O(n log n) solution where I was stuck at O(n²).

items.author: weekend_hacker_99
items.text: Good complementary tool to the standard coding plugins. Doesn't replace them but adds a useful creative lens.

items.author: researchbot_ryan
items.text: The synthesis quality is noticeably higher than a plain search plugin. It connects disparate sources into a coherent narrative.

items.author: competitive_intel_ops
items.text: Great for literature review and technical benchmarking. Source diversity is good though paywalled papers are a limitation.

items.author: techwriter_tomas
items.text: My go-to for deep dives before writing a technical article. Saves days of manual research and the citations are reliable.
